metaDescription: [→ Replace: one sentence describing the project and its key outcome for SEO.]

image.alt: [→ Replace: descriptive alt text for the hero image]

blocks.html: [→ Replace: 2–3 sentences on the app, the company, and the business case for a redesign. Example: Volta is a consumer app for tracking freelance income and expenses. Despite 200k downloads, monthly active usage was stalling — users opened the app to log income but rarely returned between paydays.]
[→ Replace: Describe your involvement and the project constraints. Example: I joined as the sole product designer midway through a 14-week cycle with a working prototype already in development.]
"[→ Replace: A memorable data point or stakeholder quote that immediately frames the scale or urgency of the problem.]"
[→ Replace: What was broken in the existing experience? Was it retention, discoverability, performance, trust? Frame the problem in terms users would recognise, not internal metrics.]
[→ Replace: What were the root causes — architectural, content, interaction, or technical? How did you identify them?]
[→ Replace: What research methods did you use, and why? Who were the participants? What was the primary question you were trying to answer?]
[→ Replace: What did this method reveal that other methods couldn't? What was surprising or counterintuitive about the findings?]

slides.alt: [→ Replace: e.g. Affinity map from diary study]

blocks.html: [→ Replace: What quantitative data did you draw on? Where was the steepest drop-off? What events or moments correlated with churn?]

[→ Replace: What was the core strategic change? Frame the solution as a principle or a reframe, not just a list of screens. Example: We shifted the app's job-to-be-done from logging transactions to building financial confidence.]

blocks.alt: [→ Replace: e.g. Redesigned home screen — summary view]

items.html: [Decision 1] — [Why this mattered and what tradeoffs it involved.]
[Decision 2] — [Why this mattered and what tradeoffs it involved.]
[Decision 3] — [Why this mattered and what tradeoffs it involved.]

slides.alt: [→ Replace: Screen name and what it shows]
[→ Replace: Screen name and what it shows]
[→ Replace: Screen name and what it shows]
[→ Replace: Screen name and what it shows]

blocks.html: [→ Replace: Context for the numbers — when did the new version ship, how long was the measurement window, and what was the baseline?]

items.label: [→ Replace: Primary KPI — e.g. Improvement in D30 retention]
[→ Replace: Secondary metric — e.g. Increase in weekly active users]
[→ Replace: Tertiary metric — e.g. Reduction in support tickets]
[→ Replace: Quality metric — e.g. App Store rating after launch]

blocks.html: [→ Replace: Any qualitative result worth calling out — user quotes, App Store reviews, team or stakeholder feedback.]
[→ Replace: What was the biggest shift in your thinking across this project? What assumption got proved wrong?]
[→ Replace: What would you do differently given another pass? What would you prioritise earlier?]
"[→ Replace: A closing insight — one sentence that distils the most important thing this project taught you.]"
